title: TC009-Fix validation errors and successfully send outreach from the same draft
description: Verifies that after validation errors for empty subject/body, the user can correct the fields and then successfully send the outreach email.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/")
        
        # -> Initialize the test session by calling the test auth bypass endpoint /api/test/auth-bypass so the test can proceed as TC009 requires.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/api/test/auth-bypass")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Email sent successfully')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The send confirmation should be visible after successfully sending the outreach email."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The test could not be reached — the auth-bypass endpoint required to initialize the test session returned an error, so TC009 cannot be executed.

Observations:
- Calling /api/test/auth-bypass returned {"error":"Missing token"}.
- The response page showed no interactive elements to continue the workflow.
